notes: Receipt gas covers two controlled match txs, five on-chain cancel txs, score request, settle, and two claim txs. Off-chain submits/cancels had no on-chain gas.
Oracle callback receipt is recorded separately and is not counted as operator-controlled POL gas.

caveats: Two initial MM audit-poll PositionWithoutCommitment errors were observed from carry-over contest-33 positions and a fresh local MM state; they did not create persistent divergence, duplicate fill telemetry, or non-zero final exposure.
Reconnect coverage used a separate v0.6.2 watcher through a local drop proxy so the full-soak watcher could remain never-reconnected for the passive-expiry regression row.

MM persisted-cursor catchup resume across process restart was not exercised: current MM design persists the cursor but not the baseline book, so a process restart with only a cursor intentionally triggers resume-without-baseline cold-restart/fresh-snapshot fallback. The artifact treats the restart leg as a restart-safety fallback proof, not as proof of process-level cursor+baseline resume.

teamIdentity.away: Milwaukee Brewers / underdog by reference implied probability during run
teamIdentity.home: Athletics / favorite by reference implied probability during run

verdict.label: GREEN / PASS complete with postgame lifecycle claimed
verdict.reason: v0.6.2 corrected the long-running watcher passive-expiry liveCommitmentCount regression and the postgame lifecycle is now complete: MLB final was Athletics 7, Milwaukee Brewers 5; contest 33 scored, speculation 23 closed with home/lower as winner, both controlled winning lower positions were claimed, final claim-all dry-runs are empty, and public/orderbook/watch/process zero checks remain clean.
verdict.restartScope: process restart proved documented cold-restart fallback safety; mid-session cursor catchup is covered by the separate reconnect probe; process-level cursor+baseline resume is not implemented in current MM design.

restartSafety.interpretation: The MM process restart did not exercise persisted-cursor catchup resume. Current MM design persists only the cursor; the baseline book is in memory, so a process restart with only a cursor triggers the documented resume-without-baseline cold-start fallback before rebuilding from a fresh snapshot.
restartSafety.safetyOutcome: fallback rebuilt cleanly, health hold entered and cleared, stale quotes were cancelled, divergence remained zero, and duplicate fill telemetry was not observed.
